The household-side DAF account at Fidelity Charitable was opened four years ago on the bunched-charitable-giving tax-planning strategy stacking multiple years of itemized-charitable-deductions into a single tax-year contribution-and-grant cycle ahead of the standard-deduction threshold band on the household-adult's tax-return-side itemize-versus-standard-deduction-election decision-tree. The contribution-processing step plus the appreciated-securities-acceptance-and-liquidation cycle at Fidelity Charitable's investment-management-side step plus the household-side itemized-deduction substantiation on the contribution-acknowledgment letter on the household-side tax-return-preparation file all need to clear the five-PM Eastern processing-window today on the DAF year-end contribution-processing cycle. A missed-window outcome on the contribution-acceptance cycle forfeits the current-tax-year itemized-charitable-deduction for the household-side contribution pushing the donation characterization into the next-tax-year contribution cycle on the bunched-charitable-giving rolling tax-planning strategy with the household-side tax-planning-rollup recalibration on the projected adjusted-gross-income-and-charitable-deduction interaction onto next-year's tax-return cycle.
